title: mzspec project configuration
description: Per-project config that drives the mzspec gate-resolver and ship pipeline. mzspec layers on top of OpenSpec and reuses the openspec/ folder for artifacts.

name.description: Short gate id, shown in the plan/evidence.

cmd.description: Shell command. `{dir}` is substituted with the touched unit directory.

mzspecVersion.description: The mzspec release this project was installed/pinned from.

openspec.description: Where OpenSpec artifacts live; mzspec writes evidence/handoffs into this tree.

toolchains.description: One entry per toolchain. Declaration ORDER matters: on an equal-length path-prefix tie, the first-declared toolchain wins (declare `go` before `py` to keep a Go module authoritative over a same-named Python member).

dirs.description: Repo-relative package/module directories owned by this toolchain. Longest-prefix match wins.

bench.description: Optional 'free ladder' that runs as its own unit and is auto-added when a trigger toolchain is touched.

prefix.description: Path prefix that marks a bench change (e.g. 'benchmarks/').

alsoWhenToolchains.description: Toolchains whose presence also triggers the bench ladder (e.g. ['py']).

metaPrefixes.description: Doc/spec/config-only path prefixes — no build/test gate beyond the always-gates.

always.description: Gates that run for every change (e.g. `openspec validate`).

migration.description: Optional DB-migration always-gate, triggered when a touched path matches `pattern`.

pattern.description: JS RegExp source matched against each touched path.

gatesDir.description: Where this project's own gate scripts live (see the gate plugin contract).

templatesDir.description: Where this project's optional, skill-like task-planning templates live (default openspec/templates). Each is a <name>/TEMPLATE.md planning guide consulted during planning; see the templates contract.

customGates.description: Project gate plugins. Each runs when its `when` matches the diff.

touches.description: Path prefix that must be present in the diff.

invariants.description: Project hard-invariants surfaced to the code-review / security skills.

taskSources.description: Backlog sources for /opsx:task-pull|push|log. The first `enabled` one is the default (override with --source).

taskStatusMap.description: Optional per-adapter status mapping. gh-issues: normalized->tokens ('+label','-label','state:closed','state:open'). mello-cli: { columns: { <status>: <columnName> } }. Adapters use sane defaults when omitted.
